chief_complaint: A 56-year-old man presented with recurrent melena.
history: The patient has a history of recurrent melena. He underwent an esophagogastroduodenoscopy which revealed large fundic gastric varices without esophageal varices. Cross-sectional imaging identified a wandering spleen displaced into the pelvic cavity with venous congestion but no evidence of liver disease. He had a previous endoscopic cyanoacrylate injection for hemostasis, but it provided only temporary relief.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy revealed large fundic gastric varices without esophageal varices. Cross-sectional imaging identified a wandering spleen displaced into the pelvic cavity with venous congestion but no evidence of liver disease.
correct_diagnosis: Wandering spleen with splenic vein torsion and sinistral portal hypertension
differentials: Wandering spleen with splenic vein torsion and sinistral portal hypertension
Gastric varices due to liver cirrhosis
Gastric varices due to portal vein thrombosis
Gastric varices due to splenic vein thrombosis
Gastric varices due to non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) use
